plan: Step 1: Create `src/ui/info-button.ts` ‚Äî Export a `createInfoButton()` function that creates a `<button>` element with an 'i' icon (CSS-only, no image assets). The button must have `pointer-events: auto`, aria-label for accessibility, and a class for styling. It appends itself into the `#overlay-ui` container.

Step 2: Create `src/ui/info-modal.ts` ‚Äî Export a `createInfoModal()` function that creates a modal overlay with a concise explanation of 404 Museum ('Every page is a website that never existed. Refresh for a new one.'). The modal includes a close button (√ó), closes on overlay backdrop click, closes on Escape key, and uses `pointer-events: auto`. Modal is hidden by default (display: none) and toggled via a `show()`/`hide()` API.

Step 3: Create `src/styles/info-button.css` ‚Äî Style the floating info button: fixed to bottom-right corner (e.g., `bottom: 24px; right: 24px`), circular shape, subtle semi-transparent background, pointer-events: auto, hover/focus states. Must be visible at all viewport sizes (use reasonable sizing like 44px minimum touch target for mobile).

Step 4: Create `src/styles/info-modal.css` ‚Äî Style the info modal: centered card on a semi-transparent backdrop, max-width constrained, padding, close button positioned top-right, readable typography, responsive (works on small viewports). Animate open/close with CSS transitions (opacity + transform).

Step 5: Wire up in `src/main.ts` ‚Äî Import the new CSS files and modules. After container validation, call `createInfoButton(overlayUi)` and `createInfoModal(overlayUi)`. Connect button click to `modal.show()`, modal close to `modal.hide()`.

Step 6: Create `tests/us-013-info-button.test.ts` ‚Äî Write tests following existing patterns (file-system based, reading source files): (1) src/ui/info-button.ts exists, (2) src/ui/info-modal.ts exists, (3) src/styles/info-button.css exists, (4) src/styles/info-modal.css exists, (5) info-button.css contains pointer-events: auto, (6) info-button.css positions bottom-right (bottom + right properties), (7) info-button.css has position: fixed OR is inside the fixed #overlay-ui, (8) info-modal has a close mechanism (Escape listener or close button), (9) main.ts imports info-button and info-modal modules, (10) main.ts imports info-button.css and info-modal.css, (11) TypeScript compiles cleanly, (12) Vite build succeeds.
Step 7: Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` ‚Äî Fix any issues until all pass cleanly.
testing_recommendations: Verify the button is visible and clickable at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1440px) viewport widths
Verify clicking the info button opens the modal and the modal content is visible
Verify the modal closes via the √ó button, backdrop click, and Escape key
Verify the button does not block interaction with the generated site content beneath it
Verify no console errors in dev or prod builds
Verify all existing US-001/002/003 tests still pass (no regressions)
Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test` as required by CLAUDE.md
risks: The `#overlay-ui` container uses `pointer-events: none` ‚Äî every interactive child element must explicitly set `pointer-events: auto` or clicks will pass through
Modal z-index must be higher than the info button to prevent button clicks while modal is open
Existing test regex patterns (e.g., US-003 matching `\s*
Touch target size must be at least 44√ó44px per mobile accessibility guidelines ‚Äî a small circular button could be too small on mobile if not sized carefully
CSS transitions on the modal need to handle the display:none ‚Üî display:block boundary (display is not animatable) ‚Äî use opacity + visibility or a class-based approach instead
